summary: Verified fix for Jaggery/Sugar data duplication issue. New centralized pantry items API working correctly. All 11 backend tests pass. Frontend IndianPantryTemplate loads from API and displays items correctly.
passed_tests: API /api/pantry-items/template returns correct pantry data structure with categories
API /api/pantry-items/essentials returns 22 essential items with correct units
Jaggery in essentials has unit='g' (verified fix)
Sugar in essentials has unit='g' (verified fix)
Sweeteners subcategory exists in template with Jaggery and Sugar
All template items have 'unit' and 'category' fields
Frontend search for 'Sweeteners' shows correct category
Frontend shows '9 items already in your inventory' indicator
critical_code_review_comments: SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH implemented correctly in /app/backend/data/pantry_items.py
PANTRY_TEMPLATE contains all items with category and unit fields
CATEGORY_UNITS mapping correctly assigns 'g' to 'sweeteners' category
get_item_details() correctly returns unit from CATEGORY_UNITS based on category
get_essentials_pack() uses centralized data for household essentials
Frontend IndianPantryTemplate.js correctly fetches from API instead of hardcoded data
Frontend uses item.unit from API response for each item
context_for_next_testing_agent: Pantry items centralization fix verified. New API endpoints at /api/pantry-items/* are working. Frontend IndianPantryTemplate now fetches from API. Jaggery and Sugar correctly show 'g' unit (not 'L'). Test user recipe_tester@test.com has active household with 9 inventory items.

bug_verification.original_issue: Jaggery was categorized differently in households.py (Sugar & Sweeteners) vs IndianPantryTemplate.js (Oils, Sweeteners & Condiments), causing incorrect unit assignment (L instead of g)
bug_verification.root_cause: Data duplication - pantry items were defined in multiple places with inconsistent categorization
bug_verification.fix_applied: Created SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH in /app/backend/data/pantry_items.py with new API endpoint /api/pantry-items/template. Frontend now fetches from this API.
bug_verification.verification_result: PASSED - Jaggery returns category='sweeteners' and unit='g' from all API endpoints. Frontend displays correctly.

api_tests./api/pantry-items/essentials: Both Jaggery and Sugar have unit='g'
api_tests./api/pantry-items/template: Sweeteners subcategory contains Jaggery and Sugar with unit='g'
